result: Sometimes I like to imagine our universe as a kind of cosmic library, full of countless books, some open, some still shut. Each one of us is like a reader wandering among the stacks, stumbling onto pages that tug at our curiosity. We flip through chapters of personal experience, borrow and lend stories, scribble margins of half-formed ideas. Occasionally, there are lines so beautiful we pause to read them aloud, to share with others. Other times, we tuck a new insight away quietly, knowing we’ll need it later. It’s an endless, imperfect, fascinating place—ever expanding with new narratives, whispers, and questions. And the best part? None of us ever runs out of books to explore.

It’s an endless, imperfect, fascinating place—ever expanding with new narratives, whispers, and questions. And the best part? None of us ever runs out of books to explore.
